name: Section.io Edge CDN and Engineering Platform API
what_it_does: Developer-friendly edge platform with REST API — enabling engineering teams to run arbitrary HTTP reverse proxies (Varnish, NGINX, ModSecurity, OpenResty) at the edge via Docker containers, with full control over cache behavior, WAF rules, and edge logic, combined with real-time traffic analytics, GitOps deployment workflow, and integration with any CDN provider.
use_cases: Managing edge module configurations (Varnish VCL, NGINX config) via Section.io API for GitOps workflow
Deploying custom reverse proxy logic to edge via Section.io API for edge application delivery
Querying real-time edge analytics and request metrics via Section.io API for performance monitoring
Configuring WAF rules and DDoS protection via Section.io ModSecurity API
Integrating Section.io with existing CI/CD pipeline via API for automated edge deployment
not_for: Non-engineers (Section.io requires strong knowledge of Varnish/NGINX for configuration)
Simple CDN without customization (use Cloudflare or Fastly for simpler CDN needs)
Video streaming (use Cloudflare Stream or Mux for video delivery)
best_when: A platform engineering team needs to run custom reverse proxy logic (Varnish VCL, NGINX, ModSecurity) at the edge — with the Docker container model allowing any HTTP middleware to be deployed as edge compute.
avoid_when: You don't have Varnish/NGINX expertise, need a turnkey CDN without engineering overhead, or primarily deliver video content.

auth.notes: API key from Section.io account. Basic auth or bearer token. Account-scoped access. No webhooks. REST API for application and environment management. GitOps workflow: edge config in Git repository, deployed via push. Docker-based edge modules (Varnish, NGINX, ModSecurity, OpenResty, Lua). No OAuth2. No fine-grained scopes.

pricing.paid_tiers: Developer from $100/month; Business from $500/month; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Australian company (Adelaide/Melbourne). Founded 2013 by Stewart McGrath and Daniel Austin. Bootstrapped and profitable. Unique Docker-based edge compute model — any HTTP reverse proxy can run at edge. GitOps deployment (config in Git) was innovative before GitOps was a term. 100+ PoPs globally using cloud provider infrastructure. Strong in e-commerce performance optimization. Customers include Shopify merchants, media companies. Engineering-first CDN — not for non-technical users.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for application management. API key auth. Good technical documentation for Varnish/NGINX configuration. No webhooks. GitOps workflow — config pushed to Git for deployment. Australian company with GDPR compliance. Docker-based edge module model is unique but requires deep expertise. Rate limits not documented.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Application reads fully idempotent. Environment reads idempotent. Configuration reads idempotent. Deployment creation requires idempotency at GitOps layer (commit-based).
known_agent_gotchas: Varnish/NGINX expertise required — not a turnkey CDN
GitOps model means edge config is in Git — deployment via git push, not API
Docker container edge modules — each module must be properly sized for PoP resources
No webhooks — must poll for deployment status
Rate limits not documented — test before high-frequency management API calls

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2 and GDPR compliant. Multi-region data residency. API key auth. Australian company with GDPR awareness. Docker-based edge modules require container security best practices. ModSecurity WAF configuration requires security expertise. GitOps model — edge config in Git means security of Git repo is critical. TLS termination at edge exposes cleartext to Varnish/NGINX modules — configure TLS re-encryption.
